desc:
While the outside of the tomb is withered and dirty from bird droppings the
inside is more richly decorated. The walls are panelled with slates of fine
brown travertine. Bands of interwoven lines sculptured in the slates frame fine
reliefs. A tessellated floor shows a hunting horn, a symbol related to the Vala
Orome. Droppings of animals cover most of it.
name: An Ancient Tomb

desc:
The terrain changes from plains to woodland. The ground is flat, falling
slightly towards the Anduin that must flow somewhere southeast. The trees are
mostly oaks, but between the oaks some fruit-trees can be found. Some rotting
poles show that parts of this forest were a fruit garden in the past. A deep,
muddy gully blocks the way deeper into the forest northwards. To the east the
gully continues, and behind it is a windfall that looks even harder to travel.
name: Forest Corner

desc:
A dark forest looms to the east of this old farm. Yet no one seems to be living
here. The thatched roof has large holes, and the hinges have lost the grip of
the door. On the yard the weeds have filled the flower beds. A tiny roof
protects a well hole in the middle of the yard.
name: Deserted Farm by the Edge of the Wood

desc:
This stretch of land is a huge, very flat plain. Covered by the long grass that
is typical for the Anduin vale, it lacks all higher vegetation. Some small
gullies, hidden in the high grass, could be natural or ruins of some sort of
irrigation, as there is a ruined farm located to the east. A hedge of thorny
bushes protects the area to the north.
name: Flat Plains

desc:
This area is covered with rich plant life. It lacks big trees, but there are
many shrubs and bushes that grow well in the dark, rich soil. Between the bushes
is long, green grass. In the northern part of the area a few remains of fences
indicate this green land was once used as pasture. An alley of trees can be seen
in the west. A dark line at the horizon to the northeast could be a forest
border.
name: Verdant Plains

desc:
Two rows of poplars mark a stretch of land that was a well-travelled way a long
time ago. Today the road is overgrown with grass. Some of the large trees have
fallen, and some stumps mark where other trees stood. The path connected the now
ruined settlement north with the shrine south. West of the poplar rows was once
farmland; east of them are wide plains.
name: Dying Alley of Trees

desc:
Small walls and rotting wooden fences show this land was used to grow food.
Irrigated by small trenches coming from a waterhole east of it, the farmland was
very fertile. Without the caring hand of farmers the trenches have filled with
mud and the fields were claimed back by nature. The grass that has overgrown the
fields and pastures is very long and green.
name: Overgrown Farmland

desc:
The land forms a small dell which is filled by a waterhole. There is no running
water that feeds the waterhole, but it looks deep enough to be fed by ground
water. The hole is surrounded by steep slopes to the north and west and a
crumbling cliff to the south.
name: Waterhole

desc:
The soil of this land is too dry and sandy to support bushes or trees. The plant
life is poor. Most of the land is covered by thin, dried out grass whose brown
colour gives the area a monotone look. The steppes to the north look more arid,
while the land south seems to be more fertile. The way east ends at a cliff edge
over a waterhole.
name: Poor Plains

desc:
This stretch of land seems not to be fertile. The grass is brown and looks
sickly. Patches of dry, brown soil can be found amidst the grass, as well of
small islands of bare grey rocks. The land to the north seems to be very dry,
while to the south it looks more fertile. To the east is an extremely dense
hedgerow of thorny bushes.
name: Dry Plains

desc:
The naked rocks and the small patches of dried grass are covered by a reddish
brown dust. Thick clouds of the red dust quickly rear up like angry spirits at
the slightest gust of wind. Small thorny brush grows in cracks and crevices
where the dust gathers providing some shelter for the few lizards and rodents
that manage to survive here.
name: Dusty Plains

desc:
The pressure of the frequent howling winds and lack of rain for extended periods
of time has left the ground devoid of all life. A few skeletal tree trunks,
polished by the wind and the sand are the only signs that life ever existed
here. Sharp rocks protruding through the dry soil make passing through the land
difficult.
name: Dead Plains

desc:
The brown, dried grass barely manages to cover the ground. Stretches of jagged
rocks and hard packed dirt where the wind has carried away the fertile soil lie
like open sores across the plains. To the east the sores grow larger and more
frequent.
name: Dry Plains

desc:
A thick carpet of grass covers the gently rolling terrain. The green grass of
the east however slowly turns drier and browner further west. Any wind will
cause waves rippling through the tall grass like a stormy sea. A few rocky
outcrops seem unnaturally still in the moving grass.
name: Windswept Plains

desc:
The vegetation in this area shifts from brown grass to green. A few rises are
covered with reddish heather. There are few rocks scattered over the grassland
and the heathery hills providing shelter for insects and small animals. The
plain continues in all directions, with some ruins visible to the south. A deep
ditch, maybe an ancient drainage canal, to the east is too muddy to cross.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The terrain is marked by rows of gently rolling hills covered by tall, dense
grass. The land is treeless, but a dark line on the eastern horizon could be a
forest. No traces of settlements are in the immediate surroudings; only to the
south of this grassland a few ruins can be spotted. The Anduin is in some
distance to the southeast.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Only the hardiest of grasses and the occasional stunted shrub manage to eke out
an existence on the poor soil. Conditions seem to improve towards the east and
south though, where a wider variety of grasses and weeds thrive. The layer of
soil appears thicker there as well with fewer rocky crags emerging from the
field of grass.
name: Arid Plains

desc:
Only the stone foundations of what was once a small, but prosperous village
remains. The stone outlines of three large barns and a dozen of smaller
buildings can be seen sticking up through the tall grass. The buildings were
arrayed in a circle around a central yard, now swallowed by the surrounding
grasslands. The most prominent feature of the settlement is the wide well at the
centre of the yard.
name: Ruined Village

desc:
A few hardy tufts of short grass dot the otherwise hard and dry ground. Low
stone fences mark the borders of once arable fields where wheat and rye was
grown. A few stalks still stand between the grass. Traces of shallow ditches
leading south suggests the fields were once irrigated. A high, strong hedge of
thornbushes blocks the way west and gives a little shelter from the wind.
name: Drought-stricken Farmland

desc:
Atop a tiny hill sits the charred and blackened ruin of what was once a small
manor. Once surrounded by a belt of beautiful orchards and farmland it is now
the centre of dry badlands to the northeast, and grass covered plains in all
other directions. The manor itself was burnt to a husk a long time ago and the
erosion of wind and rain has slowly begun to wash the black off the scorched
stone.
name: Blackened Ruins

desc:
This stretch of land was farmland in a long forgotten past. Today the small
fields are overgrown by the typical long, brown grass. Stones and small trenches
that marked the borders of the fields can still be found everywhere. The most
prominent features are the strong hedges of thornbushes that protected the
fields to the north and east.
name: Abandoned Fields

desc:
A heavy blanket of dust covers a multitude of broken furniture and discarded
household items, which clutter the room. Along the southern wall piles of broken
crockery shows where shelves once stood, now long since rotted away. In a corner
the remnants of a small campfire and a neat stack of splintered wood suggests
travellers have sought shelter here in the past.
name: Littered Cellar

desc:
Inside the well a faint glow can be detected from the fluorescent mosses and
algae growing here. The moist, slippery walls are made out of granite bricks,
expertly fitted together without mortar. Rusted iron rungs, placed a foot apart,
provide a slippery way out of the well. The two feet of water at the bottom is
cold and crystal clear.
name: Bottom of the Well

desc:
Boundless fields of unbroken green stretch forth in all directions. Though made
up from several different types of grass they are all rather short giving the
appearance of a well kept lawn. Only the outlines of a forest to the east and
the silhouette of the Misty Mountains to the west break the monotony.
name: Emerald Plains

desc:
The plains here change to a dry moor. Heather covers the land, colouring it
red-brown. Small drainage trenches show that once someone tried to change the
moor into fields. A broad and very muddy trench to the west makes it impossible
to go there. To the south is a hedge of dense bushes that looks like a field
border.
name: Heather Covered Moor

desc:
West of a dark forest lies a soft sloping moorland. Bushes of heather change
with sandy spots and patches of brown grass. The sparse vegetation indicates
that the soil is not very fertile. There are only little traces of larger
animals, but numerous rabbit holes.
name: Heather-covered Moor

desc:
The ground becomes more and more uneven and some big stones lie scattered around
as if some giant had played with rocks out of boredom. Somewhere in the west are
flattened fields where the stones have been removed with great effort and a
small line is visible behind the rocks. The line turns out to be a hedge wall
that is too high to jump over. It is grown of thousands of bushes with sharp,
protruding thorns planted close to each other. To the south a similar hedge
provides some shadow. Its rampant thorns and leaves and bits of small branches
sticking out of the green and brown mass make the hedge an impenetrable
obstacle.
name: Corner of Hedges

desc:
Huge old oaks with dense foliage are the most common trees in this forest, but
some poplars and chestnuts grow between them. The trees branches and leaves form
a dense roof that blocks most light, making it difficult for smaller plants to
grow on the ground. The underwood is sparse with only a few ferns and some
thorny shrubs at the forest borders. A huge windfall blocks all travel south.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
West and south from a dark oak forest, these plains are covered with dense
grass. Clusters of bushes and small trees are scattered over the grassland. To
the south some ruins, probably of a farm, can be seen. A huge windfall creates a
barrier that prevents travel northwards.
name: Plains near a Forest

desc:
This is the border of a huge wood. It looks like that the trees that are lacking
on the plain land to the west have gathered here to form a dark, hostile looking
forest. Most of the trees are oaks with trunks that are more black than brown.
The forest has grown wild, and obviously has never seen the caring hand of a
forester.
name: Border of the Dark Forest

desc:
This woodland looks very ancient and dark. Formed by huge oak trees with
wide-spread branches, it looks like nobody ventured in it before. The ground is
covered by a thick carpet of rotting leaves and fallen branches. The smell of
rotting plant material is in the air, adding to the unpleasant atmosphere of the
forest.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Deeper in the forest almost no light reaches the ground. The ancient oak trees
loom dark and musty. The ground is soft from layers of fallen leaves rotting
away. No brushwood is under the trees, but a few mushrooms grow on the gnarled
roots.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Ancient oak trees dominate in this forest. Fallen trees, stumps and roots make
strolling around difficult. A few gaps in the dense ceiling of branches and
leaves allow the sun to reach some spots on the ground. Bushes and vines grow in
these spots. Windfalls to the south and west block the way.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
A dense roof of leaves and branches covers this place. The forest is formed by
thick, old-looking oak trees with moss-covered trunks. There are very few
smaller plants; it looks like the light that reaches the ground is not
sufficient to support more plant life.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
A dark tunnel ends here, to a strange, wooden pillar at the roof of the tunnel.
The dark tunnel continues towards the east, hiding its secrets in the lurking
darkness. The cavern floor consists of solid rock, on which thin streams of
water run down to the east, making the mossy surface slippery. Some lichen grow
on the walls of this narrow passage.
name: Below the Tunnel Entrance

desc:
Some old storage chests lie on the ground on a flat part of gently descending
tunnel. The floor is covered with droppings of large animals, making the surface
a mess. Strange carvings decorate the northern cavern wall here. The floor and
the walls are overgrown by some weak moss, and a few small mushrooms grow in the
shadows.
name: Descending Tunnel

desc:
A wet and mossy tunnel steeply rises to the west, offering no hold for climbing
on its slippery surface. Small streams of clear water run down the slope. The
bottom here is covered with fungi, moss and mushrooms, and large, sharp rocks
peeking from below them. Some broken bones of a human skeleton lie on the
ground, presenting the fate of an unfortunate adventurer.
name: Bottom of a Slimy Pit

desc:
Under the dark forest opens an even darker cavern. The walls consist of earth,
and only the roots of the large trees prevent this hole from cave-ins. The
ground is covered with rotting plant material formed in comfortable sleeping
places for the dangerous predators that seem to live here.
name: The Cave under the Forest

desc:
This room has the shape of a beehive, and its six walls are decorated in a
honeycomb pattern. In the centre of the chamber a huge, richly decorated stone
chest is set. Everything is covered by a fine film of ice crystals. The air in
this crypt is extremely cold.
name: A Cold Crypt

desc:
A large stone-laid courtyard extends here. To the north stands a guard post
surveying the court and its travellers. A passage in the south part of the court
leads further into the castle. A fountain has been placed in the centre of the
huge open space. The fountain has been completely constructed out of bone -
human bone. The liquid that comes dripping out of a number of different empty
eye sockets and gaping yaws is not water.
name: Fountain Court

desc:
A tight passage leads away from the courtyard to the north further into the
Angmarian castle. No natural light source gives guidance in between the tall
iron-clad walls. They are no more than six feet apart from each other. The
well-worn floor is composed of large flat stones laid in a straight pattern, the
work of someone very skilled as no joints are visible in between the stones.
name: Passage

desc:
The passage twists and turns as it goes deeper into the castle, always with a
small incline. It is quite narrow here: only a few feet separate the high walls
from each other. Along the walls, there hang mighty iron shields, dark and
threatening. There is a slight draft in the dry air, moving the warmth upwards
and dragging with it the smell of decomposition and death.
name: Passage

desc:
The tunnel, narrow and quite steep, ends here. Glancing down the tunnel, one can
perceive a steep descent, where slippery steps have been worn out by the
constant stomping of metal-clad shoes. The hallway continues north and west,
arrow loops facing into the passage glare along both sides of it. Its owners,
apparently, were more afraid of things inside the castle rather than outside.
name: Descending Stairs

desc:
This chamber can host a small force which could defend the courtyard south of
here. Solid walls contain a set of hard beds and a few places to sit at.
Otherwise, there is no interior furniture or decoration except for a hanging on
the northern wall. It depicts a grand man riding a fell beast commanding a large
force in battle. A silent whisper floats around, chilling the bones and
instilling fear in one's heart.
name: Guard Chamber

desc:
Imbued with age, menace, dread, and oppressing magnificence, lavish tapestries
of days past hang along the sides of the hallway. While their opulence is
seemingly misplaced in this castle of walking horrors, its content matter would
augment well its owner's ambition: Morgoth sitting stoutly on his subterranean
throne in Angband, a vision of terror clad in black armour, iron-crowned, and
casting a shadow like a storm cloud.
name: Passage

desc:
Trajectories of dull afterglow slash across the middle of the room from torches
that have burned here in a different time and different age. These ghostly
reminders hang at the edge of one's vision, illuminating the lack of furnish in
this room. Where the walls meet the floor, rusty stains line the perimeter where
weapons and arrow racks were once bolted. An arched doorway, its scrollwork
intact, leads out into the passage to the east.
name: Guard Chambers

desc:
The austere interior of the castle is just a perforation in the dark stone of
Carn Dum. Its walls are barely worked, while its floor is polished smooth - more
so by the foot traffic, than by whatever craftsmen built this place eons ago.
Strange odours linger in the corners of the passage, a mixture of death and the
dampness permeating the walls.
name: Passage

desc:
The glum atmosphere of the castle seems to seep out the walls themselves here.
Their irregular surfaces accommodate a pair of rudimentary square alcoves,
carved opposite one another. Solidified remains of candle wax drip,
stalactite-like, toward the floor. The corridor is quite wide here, but its
walls narrow quickly above, where they come together at an angle that is
unnatural to anything man-built.
name: Passage

desc:
Uneven, the walls of the passage present their unfriendly empty faces to the
interior. This appears to be a natural fissure that narrows at the top. The
walls must join somewhere above, but the merge exists too far up for it to be
distinguishable with any regular illumination. Fading arrow marks are painted
red on the walls, pointing north.
name: Passage

desc:
This wide low crevasse in the dark stone forms a bend in the passage deep in the
keep of Angmar. The castle is perched next to a grand mountain range, and its
back rests against it, and, in some cases like this, resides within it. Among
the ceiling, the walls, and the floor, only the latter is carved, so that it may
be smooth enough for visitors and residents to walk upon without stumbling in
the eternal ethereal murkiness of the castle's passages.
name: Passage

desc:
Fresh air, at a premium in the stagnant bowels of this loathsome tower, filters
through a small square window in the northern wall. It tapers slightly to the
top, so its perfectly square iron cover does not fit tightly, allowing for a
robust draft. A stone bench, as much a part of the rock of the citadel as the
table in front of it, sits across from it. Blood streaks the bench, the table,
and the stone floor, but wither it came from one can no longer tell.
name: Guard Chambers

desc:
Cavernous corridors in the heart of the Angmar's dread-castle abruptly come to
an end, turning into a small guard chamber to the west. A small, crudely painted
window to the north lights up the hallway sufficiently to make out a grand
tapestry on the opposite wall. The tapestry depicts the treacherous ice of
Helcaraxe, and Morgoth's crossing of it with his hideous companion, Ungoliant
the Gloomweaver. Its blood-red background is made of no regular paint, and the
fabric itself still seethes of malice from the evils that it took to create it
and its subject matter.
name: Passage

desc:
Human skulls in clay insets stud the low part of the walls, serving as
candle-holders. The dark porous stone of the walls seems to seep darkness, with
which the vile blue candles cannot keep up. A red tapestry takes the entire
southern wall. In its centre, Ungoliant consuming the two White Trees is
depicted; a dreadful visage made more so by the background colouring around it,
that is soaked in what is distinctly blood.
name: Passage

desc:
Heavy walls of black cold stone lean in over the passage, almost as if they
would collapse at any moment. White grinning skulls adorn the walls, their eye
sockets softly glowing with crimson red light. The ground has been trodden for
many centuries, its surface worn down by countless iron-shod feet. Two huge
pillars stand on each side of the exit leading westwards, with small dragons of
marble slithering up and down the massive colonnades.
name: Checkpoint

desc:
Decorative animal skulls, mouths open, are sitting here in clay insets a few
hand-spans above the floor. Short stubby candles, flaring on and off with
ghost-flames, are standing in their screaming throats. They illuminate little,
for the black stone walls are so opaque, they seem to consume any light that
touches them. A blood-red tapestry hangs along the northern wall, into which
pictorials of human and elven famine and pestilence are woven around the scene
of Morgoth killing Finwe with rather gory details.
name: Passage

desc:
The chamber is square and dark, plain and unadorned except for the great stair
that ascends into the darkness high above, wide and sweeping as it climbs toward
the higher reaches of the keep. The railings of the black stone stair are carved
and shaped, making the pillars resemble twisted and crippled men, crying in
agony and pain, as they carry the weight of the steps.
name: Ornate Stairs

desc:
Dark cold stone walls rise some yards up, where a murky wooden roof shields the
inhabitants of the castle from the forces of nature. Six beds protrude from the
western wall. They are constructed out of the same dark stone as the walls, but
no hint remains if they were ever in use. In the middle of the room, there
stands a low table carved of one large piece of stone placed on top of two
smaller stones. Around the table stand four wooden chairs. The air is dry and
chilly. Where there once was a wooden door in the southern wall, there is now
only a dark gaping man-sized hole.
name: Guard Chambers

desc:
Small and dark, this triangular chamber is more like an alcove in which a few
guards can stand, ever vigilant. Blackened stone walls loom above, leaning out
towards the stairs, meeting high above. They almost seem to physically push down
on the room, the cold stones seemingly moving inexorably to crush whatever or
whoever that comes unaware in its path.
name: Guard Chambers

desc:
A small chamber, not quite circular, is irregular in a way that is slightly
unsettling. Unadorned and dark, the room seems to focus all attention at the
portal in the eastern wall, shaped like the great gaping maw of one of the
ancient dragons. Beyond the opening lies a black chamber, whose deep darkness
cannot be pierced by mortal eyes. Only a faint, rustling sound can be heard,
almost like slow, deep breaths.
name: Before the Dragon Head

desc:
Shrouded in shadows, the room is dominated by a large black throne, looming high
above the floor on a polished black stone dais. Before the dais is a large open
area without any kind of ornaments or structures, just cold, dark and unyielding
stone.
name: The Black Throne

desc:
A natural shelter has formed between and under giant boulders leaning against
each other. Being surrounded by rocks and the densest bramble thicket, this weed
covered area is well-hidden from anyone passing through these wild borderlands.
There is a small path between the bushes leading to a hill, and a murky tunnel
which leads deeper into the ground.
name: Tunnel below the Jagged Boulders

desc:
Inside the hill, the twisting tunnel opens up into a circular cavern. The ground
is formed of a mixture of bedrock and loose gravel, and is littered by red
stains, and some old and some apparently not so old bones. There are visible
tracks on the floor, like something heavy has moved back and forth inside this
underground tunnel complex. The steep walls are of pure rock, and contain small
cracks and tiny ledges. Far above, the cavern walls seem to get closer to each
other, before their faint shapes disappear into a total darkness.
name: High, Shady Cavern

desc:
Built with defence in mind, the cavern is like a small bottleneck, with a low
floor and close walls, giving an obvious disadvantage to anyone who happens to
find themselves here. A steep slope on the south wall marks the only exit to the
outside world from this underground network.
name: Hidden Entrance

desc:
Many crudely built iron cages with tied gates line the far wall of this tiny,
filthy cavern. Tufts of fur and piles of droppings litter the floor, giving very
little clean walking space and adding to the musty odour of animals that
emanates from here.
name: Warg Kennels

desc:
A huge bonfire marks the centre of this room, surrounded by rickety chairs and
tables. Bones litter the western floor, perhaps from tortuous sacrifices and
forced fighting among the slaves for amusement and ceremonies. On the eastern
wall, raised upon a pedestal, a crude stone chair that is probably reserved for
the warchief sits next to a small well. Along the western wall there are many
torches flanking a huge, heavy gate. It appears to use some sort of a chain
mechanism to open it.
name: Morbid Throne Hall

desc:
This chamber connects to the vast hall to the south and entry is blocked only by
a curtain that stands to give a little privacy to the owner. The chamber
contains three messy beds that are placed next to a weapons rack along the
northern wall.
name: Warchief's Quarters

desc:
Carved into the stone, this chamber is completely barren and desolate. No
torches light it, and no furnishings can be found on the dusty floor. The only
way out is through the heavy gate to the east which appears to use some sort of
a chain mechanism to open it. The chamber is probably used to house slaves and
prisoners of the inhabitants.
name: Slave Chambers

desc:
The underground tunnel ends in a small, stinking cave. Among the dark rocks,
numerous bones and some half-eaten corpses lies an open area which is clearly a
residency of some large beast. A small, worn-looking tunnel leads to the north
on the ground level, and judging by the occasional bats that flit above, there
is another passageway above, twisting upwards through the darkness.
name: Stinking Lair

desc:
A smooth, narrow ledge runs along the rock wall near the cavern's ceiling,
forming a dangerous path over the dark emptiness where one wrong step can cost a
lot. On the southern end, the passageway transforms into a steep tunnel which
leads sharply downwards into the rock, while on the northern side of the cavern
the rocky path ends up into a small cave. Beyond the edge of this three feet
wide ledge only a sheer and probably deadly decline awaits.
name: On a Rocky Ledge

desc:
Surrounded by stalagmites, irregular rock formations and deep shadows of this
sinister cavern, this hidden vantage provides a view through a wide crack which
splits its rocky floor to the rocky cavern floor located below. A narrow ledge
leads southwards, crossing the cavern far above its stony bottom. The way down
from this cave is almost vertical, but possibly passable for a skilled climber.
name: Hidden Cave above the Cavern

desc:
A narrow crack in the soft and stinking soil leads further south and descends a
bit. It cannot be said for sure if the walls and floor are just soft or are
covered with a spongy, foul mass of... something. A deeply grey mist covers the
ground at the southern end of this crack. From every direction grow dark, very
unhealty roots into this space.
name: A Narrow Crack

desc:
It appears that corruption and rot have grown so strong here, that the soil
itself became victim to decay and fell apart, creating this space. Rotting roots
stick from every direction, and a thick mist hinders vision. The air feels
freezing cold, and the strong stench bites your nose.
name: Rotting Roots and Deep Mists

desc:
Scrub grass and patches of weeds carpet this area, only a few stunted, yellowing
bushes break the uniform monotony. The grass is mostly dry and wilting,
struggling to survive with little water and too much sunlight. Even the weeds
that sprouted have thin, shrivelled leaves. Despite this, a few small beech
trees have somehow managed to grow proving that nature will find a way.
name: Barren Grasslands

desc:
A narrow trail runs through the grasslands of Anduin vale. Layers of vegetation
and tightly-packed bushes crowd against the side of the path spilling their
branches onto the trail. Especially to the west many of the bushes carry sharp
thorns, which would suggest that forcing a route through the bushes would be
painful.
name: A Small Path among the Bushes

desc:
A clearly worn path continues through the green grass fields. The terrain here
is more open and lacks the dense vegetation of the surrounding areas but
provides a suitable habitat for many smaller wild animals. To the north tall
bushes grow and the vegetation seems to spill onto the path. A few stunted
junipers are dotted amongst the grass fields continue to the east towards the
river Anduin.
name: A Worn Path through Grass Fields

desc:
The path makes a turn towards the west where an old, decayed wooden gate stands
attached to crude pillars of rock. Time and weather have not been kind to the
gate or the fence surrounding it. Wooden planks, bleached to a silvery finish by
the sun, are covered in moss and hang at uneven heights where a few have broken
free from the nails securing them to the fence. Despite the deterioration none
of the gaps are big enough to allow someone to slip around the gateway.
name: A Decayed Gate

desc:
Small shrubs and colourful flowers flourish amongst the tall, green grass that
grows all around. West from here is a sturdy and tall wooden fence which
prevents any passage in that direction. To the south, a steep descent ends up in
a muddy ditch with thorny bushes growing along the edge. A small mound can be
seen east of here.
name: Grass Field bordering a Fence

desc:
Unfriendly and uninviting is probably the best way to describe this bush-filled
field. Thorny, leafless and tangled together these bushes make the going hard
and tiresome. It seems as if these plants are trying to prevent travellers from
going further. South from here the bushes form a wall of vegetation through
which it should be possible to force a path.
name: A Crowded Mass of Dense Bushes

desc:
Thorny and mostly leafless twigs are bent and twisted together as they grow from
the bushes and shrubs surrounding this clearing. These tightly interwoven plants
offer no option but for travellers to head south and even then, it would take
very careful movements to avoid being raked by the razor sharp thorns lining the
path.
name: Prickly Bushes

desc:
Tall, thorny walls of vegetation; roots, vines and bushes, tower above on the
left and right. Between the protruding sharp thorns and entangled bushes only a
small open area exists, shaded by the dark-green vegetation that surrounds the
clearing. No sky is visible through the shrubbery, no obvious exits are visible
save to the west.
name: Deep inside Foreboding Bushes

desc:
An old wooden fence surrounds this decaying farmland where weather and time have
taken their toll on the planks of the fence. To the north many boards have
fallen to the ground from the fence and through these narrow gaps dense and
thorny bushes grow. The trail continues south from here where an old habitat of
some kind can be seen in the distance. To the east stands an old, decaying gate.
name: Overgrown Trail through Farmland

desc:
The trail runs north and south entering the front yard of a farmhouse located to
the west from here. The doorway of the house lies in ruin and the door itself
now languishes beneath a heap of debris in front of the home. The debris
consists of crumbled rubble and rotten beams making you wonder just how stable
the remaining structure is. In the yard an old, dried-up well indicates that no
one has used it for many years. East of here is an old wooden fence preventing
travel in that direction.
name: A Trail in Front of a Decayed Farmhouse

desc:
This field has clearly been used to rear cattle. A tall wooden fence lines the
edge of the field the west and to the east. Many bushes and shrubs grow in the
south which would suggest that travelling there would be tiresome. There is an
opening on the eastern fence with an arch over a gateway. Two doors stand tall
within the gateway secured by sturdy, metal latch. The trail continues to the
east through the gateway and to the north towards a farmhouse in the distance.
name: A Stockyard

desc:
A massive, latched gateway with a wooden arch on top of it has been built here.
The purpose of such a large gateway is not clear but the exceptionally thick
wood used in the construction mean it would be capable of containing even the
largest of animals. Leading directly from the gateway to the south, a prominent
path stretches away as far as the eye can see.
name: An Old, Latched Gateway

desc:
This path is wide and easy to follow as it heads to the north and south. Many
thorny bushes and treelike shrubs crowd the edge of the path offering no way
through to the west while deep, swampy waters prevent travel to the east. There
is little option but to stay on the path where some faint animal tracks can be
seen.
name: A Path in the Brush

desc:
The path takes a sharp turn as it goes around some bushes to the north-west and
avoids marshlands to the east. This part of the path has many thin tracks
crossing it where wild animals have trampled their own routes through the brush.
There is some vegetation growing around and among it some branches that have
dropped from the bushes.
name: A Turn in the Path

desc:
A thin line of trees grows to the south, behind which ever taller and denser
trees appear to huddle until it finally expands into a dark and murky forest.
The forest borderline continues to the east towards river Anduin and is filled
with wildlife, from insects and maggots to small animals like birds and
squirrels. This forest borders a bending trail just north and west. As the trees
here grow quite loosely, light has no problems penetrating the patch canopy and
it is still possible to travel mounted.
name: Border of a Musty Forest

desc:
The dense grass is flecked with streaks of green and yellow with only an
occasional flower patch breaking up the monotony and attracting some insects. A
few dispersed trees are dotted around the landscape, each specimen spreading its
canopy of healthy, green leaves as wide as possible to catch the sunlight. This
part of the land is bordered by forest to the south and east.
name: Dense Grassland

desc:
Green grass, small bushes and wild flowers grow around the area. The soil seems
to be well suited for this kind of growth, soft and damp. To the north high
grass can be seen and to the west the flatlands continue. Some tracks made by
wildlife can be seen on the soft spots of the ground.
name: Grass-filled Plains

desc:
Here in the deeper parts of the forest, no light can make its way through the
thick foliage. No plants are therefore visible on the ground, only a few
mushrooms grow on the tree roots. There seem to be some nests of birds beneath
the treetops as chirping can be heard from above.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
A big forest begins here, stretching out to the east. A great variety of trees,
including oak, pine and beech, grow at the forest border. Deeper in the forest,
oaks begin to prevail. The trees seem prosperous and healthy. The soil is soft
and nutritional since the dead trees in this forest are left to rot.
name: Edge of the Forest

desc:
The environment here is filled with lush verdure. This is the border of the
plains. Some trees can be sighted in the southern and eastern parts of this
area. Behind those, a seemingly large forest begins. The soil seems soft and
damp, very suitable for all the bushes and wild flowers growing in it.
name: Plains Close to the Forest

desc:
The ground here is filled with grass and smaller bushes. It looks like a perfect
place for cattle to graze. The land slopes softly to the east. Looking to the
west, the Misty Mountains are still visible on the horizon, like guardians
watching over the entire area.
name: Grass-filled Plains

desc:
The fields of short grass that carpets the moor is dotted by small clusters of
bushes, mainly juniper. Patches of heather surround the bushes like lilac
islands on a sea of green. There are numerous rabbit holes all over the area.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
The ground is covered in thick, decayed branches from the bushes and some
tree-leaves. An old, deteriorating path runs through the area heading away to
the east and south. At times animal tracks can be seen on it, while in other
places the small path has totally disappeared under the vegetation and litter.
Thick, dark bushes loom over the path to the north as if trying to take over the
path. Some sparse pine trees can be seen southeast.
name: Perishing Path among Bushes

desc:
Green and yellow grass grows high in this fertile field. The tall grasses
swaying to every breath of wind entering the fields. The terrain is quite level
and the ground here feels solid. Only a lone oak tree is standing among the
grass and offers very little shelter. These fields seem to continue as far as
the eye can see to the north and west.
name: High Grass Field

desc:
Nurtured by the sun, wind and rain these fields are rich with dandelions,
willowherbs, nettles and pasque flowers. Rich with colour and life this sea of
flower is a sight for the sore eye. To the east, high grass begins to grow and
to the south, grasslands that are more barren can be seen.
name: Flowery Fields

desc:
Long, lush grass grows in abundance on the plains that continue for miles to all
directions. The ground undulates up and down a bit creating small knolls and
grassy mounds in otherwise flat fields. There are swathes of dandelions,
daisies, clover and other flowers among the grass, spotting the landscape with
bright colourful dots. A small forest is visible far to the south-east.
name: Grass-filled Plains

desc:
Waist-high grass grows here coming to an abrupt end to the east where a thick
wall of bushes begin. The grass here grows so densely that it is impossible to
see through it to the ground beneath, but it feels solid enough to travel. These
grass fields continue to the north and south but the bushes growing to the east
would be impossible to penetrate.
name: High Grass Field

desc:
Solid rocky ground rises up from the high grass fields to the south. The ground
climbs sharply to the east blocking the way there. Stunted grass grows where
cracks in the rock allow their roots to reach the soil below. Some debris lies
scattered around the terrain and the only signs of animals are birds
occasionally flying over the plains. The rocky ground continues to the north.
name: Rocky Plain

desc:
Small hills rise all around and the rocky terrain is covered with broken stone.
Where grass grows in this desolate plain, it is burned yellow by a relentless
glare from the sun. Nothing more could ever grow here. To the north, these
plains descend into grassland covered in very high grass and some trees.
name: Rocky Plain

desc:
Small bushes and shrubs grow all around. The terrain rises slightly here and
there and some signs of small animals can be seen. To the west, the Misty
Mountains lie, surrounded by clouds, rising in the distance. Even without the
sun, their sheer presence casts a huge shadow over the plains. The flatlands
continue to the south and east.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
The small clearing inside the thicket is surrounded from all directions by tall,
dark-green bushes, which seem to stretch their branches, leaves and twigs to
conquer any remaining open space. No sunlight shines here. Numerous sharp thorns
and even the small, narrow leaves on them have razor-like edges with little
points on irregular intervals. There are some small openings between the plants
to the north and south, which seem to reveal yet more bushes.
name: Thorny Bushes

desc:
This is an old, two story farmhouse. Almost half of the southern wall is missing
indicating that this wooden home has definitely seen its best times. Partially
decayed wooden stairs, the bannister completely missing, lead up to the second
floor. A fireplace stands on the western wall connected to the chimney, and a
big oaken table occupies the centre of the room. The floor of the farmhouse is
partly covered in grass. Near the northern wall there is a doorway into the
kitchen.
name: An Old Decaying Farmhouse

desc:
This is clearly a room for food preparation. Some shelves are attached to the
walls and a small kitchen table is placed against the eastern wall of the room.
Two small windows open onto a view of the front yard, and below them is a
sturdy, full metal stove which has collected some rust. On the ground in front
of the stove is a metal handle of sort, indicating possible entrance down below
the floorboards.
name: Farmhouse Kitchen

desc:
Two small beds have been placed parallel with each other on opposite walls,
these may once have been a soft and warm place to sleep but are now covered in
mildew, the sheets have not been changed for quite some time. Between the beds,
a window on the northern wall provides a view to the yard, letting light into
the room. The ceiling is in reasonable condition and still provides some shelter
from the rain. In places, hay can be seen protruding from the gaps in the wooden
beams.
name: Second Floor Bedroom

desc:
Tall, yellow grass spreads out into wide, round field. So lush is the growth
that it makes the travelling quite difficult and limits the visibility to the
immediate surroundings. To the east there is a steep rise blocking the way while
some trees and bushes are growing densely to the north.
name: High Grass Field

desc:
Dense bushes grow all around, the thick, twisted stems of neighbouring bushes
have become entangled creating a dense wall of vegetation. The bushes are high
as trees preventing the sunlight from reaching the ground and suffocating the
lesser growth around them. The air is still, stifling and so dry as to be almost
unbreathable.
name: Dark Forbidding Bushes

desc:
This is a cold earth cellar dug into the ground. The only way in or out of here
is through a small hatch in the ceiling. The northern wall is of solid rock,
which would, even in summer, cool this cellar quite well regardless of the
temperature outside. All the other walls are just soil as is the ground. A
wooden cupboard is standing against the southern wall.
name: A Food Storage in the Cellar

desc:
Tall dark-green bushes grow in abundance in this area. Partly camouflaged by the
vegetation, an old-looking shelter built from branches and moss provides partial
shelter from the weather. There is a crude bench made from a log inside, and in
front of the bench a small circle of rocks for building a fire. All in all this
rough campsite can offer some shelter from light rain and wind.
name: A Crude Shelter among the Bushes
